# Solutions: Refactor monolithic code to modular code (imputation example)

This notebook provides step-by-step solutions for refactoring a monolithic imputation script into modular code, following RAP best practices. Each step matches the corresponding exercise notebook.

```{r}
# Setup for solutions

# File paths in this markdown are relative, the code chunks that produce outputs will not run unless the working directory of the markdown is changed to the project root. To set the markdown directory, select the drop down menu next to knit at the top of the Rstudio source panel, then select to Knit Directory drop down menu and select Project Directory

library(dplyr)

devtools::load_all()

input_path = here::here("data", "inputs", "health_data.csv")
cleaned_path = here::here("data", "outputs", "cleaned", "cleaned_data.csv")
report_dir = here::here("reports")

# I/O: Read data
df = read_health_data(input_path)

# Cleaning
df_clean = clean_health_data(df)
df_clean = add_bmi_column(df_clean)

# Processing
prevalence_df = calculate_disease_prevalence(df_clean)
```

## Step 1 solution: Imputation function

Below is a function that performs imputation for missing height and weight values using group means (e.g., by gender or diagnosis), and flags which values were imputed. This approach is more robust and transparent than using overall means.
```{r}
flag_missing <- function(df, columns) {
  # Add boolean columns to flag missing values for specified columns
  for (col in columns) {
    imputed_col_name <- paste0(col, "_imputed")
    df[[imputed_col_name]] <- is.na(df[[col]])
  }
  return(df)
}

impute_by_group <- function(df, col, group_col) {
  group_means <- df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(!!rlang::sym(group_col)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(group_mean = mean(!!rlang::sym(col), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::pull(group_mean)
  result <- ifelse(is.na(df[[col]]), group_means, df[[col]])
  result <- ifelse(is.na(result), mean(df[[col]], na.rm = TRUE), result)
  result <- round(result, 1)
}

impute_height_weight <- function(df, group_col) {
  df = flag_missing(df, c("height_cm", "weight_kg"))
  df$height_cm <- impute_by_group(df, "height_cm", group_col)
  df$weight_kg <- impute_by_group(df, "weight_kg", group_col)
  return(df)
}

imputed <- impute_height_weight(df, group_col = "gender")
head(imputed, 5)
```
## Step 2 solution: Add functions to pipeline
Where you place these functions depends on the context and what makes most sense for your pipeline. However, for this exercise one appropriate solution is outlined below:

1. Place the `flag_missing`, `impute_by_group` and `impute_height_weight` function into `R/cleaning.R`. You could also have added `flag_missing` to utils as it could be used across other modules.
```{r}
# This chunk will not produce an output

# cleaning.R: Data cleaning functions for RAP pipeline

clean_health_data <- function(df) {
  #' Clean health data by handling missing values and standardising columns.
  #' @param df raw health data DataFrame.
  #' @returns df_clean cleaned health data DataFrame.
  
  # Drop rows with missing diagnosis
  df$diagnosis[df$diagnosis == ""] <- NA
  df$diagnosis[df$diagnosis == "NA"] <- NA
  df_clean <- df[complete.cases(df["diagnosis"]),]
  
  # Fill missing smoker values with 'No'
  df_clean$smoker[is.na(df_clean$smoker)] <- "No"
  
  # Ensure gender is uppercase
  df_clean$gender <- toupper(df_clean$gender)
  
  return(df_clean)
}

flag_missing <- function(df, columns) {
  # Add boolean columns to flag missing values for specified columns
  for (col in columns) {
    imputed_col_name <- paste0(col, "_imputed")
    df[[imputed_col_name]] <- is.na(df[[col]])
  }
  return(df)
}

impute_by_group <- function(df, col, group_col) {
  group_means <- df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(!!rlang::sym(group_col)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(group_mean = mean(!!rlang::sym(col), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::pull(group_mean)
  result <- ifelse(is.na(df[[col]]), group_means, df[[col]])
  result <- ifelse(is.na(result), mean(df[[col]], na.rm = TRUE), result)
  result <- round(result, 1)
  return(result)
}

impute_height_weight <- function(df, group_col) {
  df = flag_missing(df, c("height_cm", "weight_kg"))
  df$height_cm <- impute_by_group(df, "height_cm", group_col)
  df$weight_kg <- impute_by_group(df, "weight_kg", group_col)
  return(df)
}
```
2. Add the functions inside the `clean_health_data` function:
    - Add the `group_col` parameter to the clean health data function
    - Make sure to remove height_cm and weight_kg from the dropna subset.
```{r}
clean_health_data <- function(df_clean, group_col) {
  #' Clean health data by dropping rows with missing values in key columns.
  #'
  #' @param:
  #'   df: A data frame containing raw health data.
  #'   group_col: The column to group on for mean imputation.
  #'
  #' @returns:
  #'   A cleaned data frame with no missing values in critical columns.

  # Make a copy of the data frame
  df_clean <- df
  
  # Drop rows with missing values in the "diagnosis" column
  df_clean <- df_clean[!is.na(df_clean$diagnosis), ]

  # Fill missing smoker values with "No"
  df_clean$smoker[is.na(df_clean$smoker)] <- "No"

  # Ensure gender is uppercase
  df_clean$gender <- toupper(df_clean$gender)

  # Impute mean values per group for height and weight
  df_clean <- impute_height_weight(df_clean, group_col = group_col)

  return(df_clean)
}
df$diagnosis <- ifelse(trimws(df$diagnosis) == "" | df$diagnosis == "NA", NA, df$diagnosis)
  
# Example usage
cat("Missing values before imputation:\n")
print(colSums(is.na(df)))

imputed <- clean_health_data(df, group_col = "gender")

cat("Missing values after imputation:\n")
print(colSums(is.na(imputed)))
```
Running source("R/cleaning.R") in main will import the function

## Step 3 Solution: Reflection

- Group-based imputation preserves important differences in the data and improves reproducibility.
- Flagging imputed values helps with transparency and downstream analysis.
- Handling edge cases (e.g., all values missing in a group) ensures robustness.

**Extension:**
The above could be taken further to automatically detect categorical and numerical columns, applying imputation to each.  
Look at the example below:
```{r}
# for utils.R
library(dplyr
        )
is_numeric_column <- function(series) {
  return(is.numeric(series))
}

is_categorical_column <- function(series) {
  return(is.character(series))
  return(is.factor(series))
  return(is.logical(series))
}

get_column_types <- function(df) {
  numeric_cols <- names(df)[sapply(df, is_numeric_column)]
  categorical_cols <- names(df)[sapply(df, is_categorical_column)]
}
get_column_types(df)
```

```{r}
# for cleaning.R
library(dplyr)
library(rlang)

get_column_types <- function(df) {
  numeric_cols <- names(df)[sapply(df, is.numeric)]
  categorical_cols <- names(df)[sapply(df, is.character) | sapply(df, is.factor)]
  return(list(numeric = numeric_cols, categorical = categorical_cols))
}

flag_missing <- function(df, columns) {
  # Add boolean columns to flag missing values for specified columns
  for (col in columns) {
    imputed_col_name <- paste0(col, "_imputed")
    df[[imputed_col_name]] <- is.na(df[[col]])
  }
  return(df)
}  
impute_numeric_by_group <- function(df, col, group_col) {
  group_means <- df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(!!rlang::sym(group_col)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group_mean = mean(!!rlang::sym(col), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    dplyr::pull(group_mean)
  result <- ifelse(is.na(df[[col]]), group_means, df[[col]])
  result <- ifelse(is.na(result), mean(df[[col]], na.rm = TRUE), result)
  result <- round(result, 1)
  return(result)
}
impute_categorical_by_group <- function(df, col, group_col) {
  mode_function <- function(x) {
    ux <- unique(na.omit(x))
    ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
  }
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(!!sym(group_col)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(!!sym(col) := ifelse(is.na(!!sym(col)),
                                mode_function(!!sym(col)),
                                !!sym(col)))
  return(df[[col]])
}

impute_columns <- function(df, group_col) {
  column_types <- get_column_types(df)
  numeric_cols <- column_types$numeric
  categorical_cols <- column_types$categorical
  cols_to_flag <- setdiff(c(numeric_cols, categorical_cols), group_col)
  
  df <- flag_missing(df, cols_to_flag)

  for (col in numeric_cols) {
    if (col != group_col) {
      df[[col]] <- impute_numeric_by_group(df, col, group_col)
    }
  }

  for (col in categorical_cols) {
    if (col != group_col) {
      df[[col]] <- impute_categorical_by_group(df, col, group_col)
    }
  }
  return(df)
}
imputed <- impute_columns(df, "gender")
print(imputed)
```
## Step 4 Solution: Refactor Visualisation Code

Below are example functions for visualising missing values and disease prevalence using ggplot. These would go in `R/report.R`.
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

plot_missing_values <- function(df, output_path) {
  cat(paste0("bulding ", output_path, "/missing_values.png\n"))
  missing_counts <- data.frame(
  columns = names(df),
  missing = sapply(df, function(x) sum(is.na(x))))
  plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(missing_counts, aes(x = columns, y = missing)) +
    ggplot2::geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#003d59", width = 0.6)
  ggplot2::ggsave(filename = paste0(output_path, "/missing_values.png"), plot = plot)
  plot
}

# Example output
missing_values_plot = plot_missing_values(df, output_path = report_dir)
print(missing_values_plot)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

df <- read.csv(input_path)
prevalence <- calculate_disease_prevalence(df)

plot_disease_prevalence <- function(prevalence, output_path, month) {
  cat(paste0("building ", output_path, "/prevalence_", month, ".png\n"))
  prevalence <- prevalence[prevalence$month == month, ]
  plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(prevalence, aes(x = diagnosis, y = prevalence_rate)) +
    ggplot2::geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#003d59", width = 0.6)
  ggplot2::ggsave(filename = paste0(output_path, "/prevalence", month, ".png"), plot = plot)
  print(plot)
}
plot_disease_prevalence(prevalence, output_path = report_dir, month = "2025-01")
```
2. Add the functions to report.R and import ggplot2
```{r}
format_month_section <- function(month, month_df) {
  
  lines <- sprintf("## Month: %s\n", month)
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(month_df)){
    row <- month_df[i, ]
    line <- sprintf(
      "- %s: %.2f%% (%d cases)\n",
      row$diagnosis,
      row$prevalence_rate * 100,
      as.integer(row$case_count)
    )
    lines <- c(lines, line)
  }
  
  return(paste(lines, collapse = ""))
}
```

```{r}
generate_markdown_report <- function(prevalence_df, output_path) {
  f <- file(output_path, "w")
  writeLines("# Disease Prevalence Report\n\n", con = f)
  for (month in unique(prevalence_df$month)) {
    month_df <- prevalence_df[prevalence_df$month == month, ]
    writeLines(format_month_section(month, month_df), con = f)
  }
  close(f)
}
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
plot_missing_values <- function(df, output_path) {
  cat(paste0("bulding", output_path, "/missing_values.png\n"))
  missing_counts <- data.frame(
  columns = names(df),
  missing = sapply(df, function(x) sum(is.na(x))))
  plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(missing_counts, aes(x = columns, y = missing)) +
    ggplot2::geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#003d59", width = 0.6)
  ggplot2::ggsave(filename = paste0(output_path, "/missing_values.png"), plot = plot)
  print(plot)
}
df <- read.csv(input_path)
plot_missing_values(df, output_path = report_dir)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

df <- read.csv(input_path)
prevalence <- calculate_disease_prevalence(df)

plot_disease_prevalence_timeseries <- function(prevalence, output_path) {
  cat(paste0("building ", output_path, "/prevalence_timeseries.png\n"))
  
  # Remove Healthy cases
  prevalence <- prevalence[prevalence$diagnosis != "Healthy", ]
  
  # Custom color palette
  palette <- c("#4C72B0", "#55A868", "#C44E52", "#8172B2", "#CCB974", "#64B5CD")
  
  # Plot timeseries
  library(ggplot2)
  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(prevalence, aes(x = month, y = prevalence_rate, color = diagnosis, group = diagnosis)) +
    ggplot2::geom_line() +
    ggplot2::geom_point() +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = palette) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = "Disease Prevalence Over Time by Disease Category",
      x = "Month",
      y = "Prevalence Rate",
      color = "Diagnosis"
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal()
  
  ggplot2::ggsave(filename = paste0(output_path, "/prevalence_timeseries.png"), plot = p, width = 8, height = 5)
}
plot_disease_prevalence_timeseries(prevalence, output_path = report_dir)
```

```{r}
generate_markdown_report <- function(prevalence_df, raw_df, output_path) {
  
  report_file <- file.path(output_path, "disease_prevalence_report.md")
  f <- file(report_file, open = "w", encoding = "UTF-8")
  on.exit(close(f), add = TRUE)

  writeLines("# Disease Prevalence Report\n\n", con = f)
  plot_missing_values(raw_df, output_path)
  plot_disease_prevalence_timeseries(prevalence_df, output_path)
  writeLines("## Missing Values Chart", con = f)
  writeLines("![Missing Values](missing_values.png)\n", con = f)

  writeLines("## Disease Prevalence Chart", con = f)
  writeLines("![Disease Prevalence](prevalence_timeseries.png)\n", con = f)
  for (month in unique(prevalence_df$month)) {
    month_df <- prevalence_df[prevalence_df$month == month, ]
    writeLines(format_month_section(month, month_df), con = f)
  }
}
```

```{r}
generate_markdown_report(prevalence_df, df, report_dir)
```
